title: Add Steps screen + view router
deliverable: A working in-memory queue that accepts user-typed tasks via AddSteps.jsx; the app routes between 'main' and 'add' views without a routing library; submitting one step per line appends each non-empty line to the queue in order and returns to the main view.

in: View router: `view` state of union type 'main' | 'add' in App.jsx, extendable to 'chains' | 'chain-edit' | 'auth' later without structural change
AddSteps.jsx component: full-screen overlay with back button, a textarea (one step per line), and Cmd/Ctrl+Enter keyboard shortcut to submit (PRD §3.4)
Submit also available via a visible Turkish button ('Ekle' or 'Adım Ekle') for mobile users who cannot use keyboard shortcuts
On submit: split textarea value on newlines, trim each line, filter empty lines, append each as a new queue item object to the in-memory queue in order
On submit: clear the textarea and navigate back to 'main'
Replace any hardcoded/static initial array in App.jsx or useQueue.js with a mutable in-memory structure that exposes an `appendSteps(lines: string[])` function
Define and export the `appendSteps` contract so Blocks 12 (chains-load) and 14 (localstorage-persistence) can reuse it without modification
Queue item shape: `{ id: string (crypto.randomUUID()), text: string, createdAt: number (Date.now()) }`
Navigation trigger from main view: an 'Ekle' button/affordance in the action bar routes to 'add' view
Empty textarea submit (all lines empty after trim) is a no-op — no state change, no navigation
Emerald/gold inline styling consistent with the design language established in earlier blocks
out: Chains screens (Blocks 12 and 13) — 'chains' and 'chain-edit' view states are defined as valid union members but not rendered
Auth view ('auth' state) — union member defined but not rendered
localStorage persistence (Block 14) — queue lives in React state only
Editing or reordering existing queue items

files.purpose: Add `view` state ('main' | 'add' | 'chains' | 'chain-edit' | 'auth'), wire `setView` into useQueue or pass as prop, replace hardcoded queue array with state from useQueue, render AddSteps when view === 'add', pass `setView` and `appendSteps` to children
Replace static initialiser array with empty or seeded mutable state; add and export `appendSteps(lines: string[])` function that creates queue item objects and appends them to the queue array
Full-screen add-steps form: back button (navigates to 'main'), controlled textarea, Cmd/Ctrl+Enter submit handler, visible submit button, auto-focus on mount, emerald/gold inline styles
Add 'Ekle' button in the action bar that calls setView('add'); ensure Queue receives queue items from useQueue rather than any local static array

implementation_steps.description: Extend view state type in App.jsx
implementation_steps.details: Add `const [view, setView] = useState('main')` at the top of App. Define a JSDoc comment or inline comment listing all valid values: 'main' | 'add' | 'chains' | 'chain-edit' | 'auth'. This comment is the router contract; future blocks look here to understand how to navigate. Use a simple `if (view === 'add') return <AddSteps ... />` before the main render return — no switch needed yet.

implementation_steps.description: Replace hardcoded queue array in useQueue.js with mutable state
implementation_steps.details: Change the initial value of the queue state from any hardcoded array to `[]` (or retain a small seed array of 1–2 demo items behind a `DEV_SEED` flag for easy manual testing — but make it easy to remove). The key change is that the state must be writable via React's setState, not a plain module-level constant. Export the full queue array and all mutators: `{ queue, appendSteps, complete, clearDone }` (complete and clearDone may already exist from Block 4/Block 10; do not break them).

Accept `lines: string[]` (already split and trimmed by the caller). For each line, create a new item `{ id: crypto.randomUUID(), text: line, createdAt: Date.now() }`. Append all new items to the queue state in a single `setQueue(prev => [...prev, ...newItems])` call to avoid multiple re-renders. Return void. This is the canonical append contract — chains (Block 12) will call this same function.

Props: `{ onBack: () => void, onSubmit: (lines: string[]) => void }`. Internal state: `const [text, setText] = useState('')`. On mount, auto-focus the textarea via `useRef` + `useEffect`. Handle keydown on the textarea: if `(e.metaKey || e.ctrlKey) && e.key === 'Enter'`, call `handleSubmit()`. `handleSubmit`: split `text` on `\n`, trim each element, filter to non-empty strings, if result is empty array do nothing and return early, otherwise call `onSubmit(lines)` and then `onBack()`. Render: back button (← Geri or ← arrow) at top-left; heading 'Adım Ekle'; textarea with placeholder 'Her satıra bir adım yaz…'; hint text 'Cmd/Ctrl + Enter ile ekle'; a submit button 'Ekle'. All styled with inline styles using CSS vars from the project theme (--color-emerald-bg, --color-gold, etc.).

In App.jsx, destructure `appendSteps` from `useQueue()`. Add: `if (view === 'add') return <AddSteps onBack={() => setView('main')} onSubmit={(lines) => { appendSteps(lines); setView('main'); }} />`. The `setView('main')` in onSubmit is redundant with AddSteps calling onBack, but having it in the parent keeps navigation logic co-located — remove the `onBack()` call from inside `handleSubmit` in AddSteps and instead rely purely on the parent's `onSubmit` prop to navigate, making the component fully controlled.

implementation_steps.description: Add 'Ekle' trigger button in Queue.jsx action bar
implementation_steps.details: Queue.jsx receives `onAddSteps: () => void` as a new prop (called `setView('add')` from App). In the action bar section (alongside any chain button placeholder), render a button with label '+ Ekle'. Style: gold border, emerald background, min touch target 44px. This button must always be visible even when the queue is non-empty — it is the primary entry point for ad-hoc task entry.

Run `npm run dev`. Verify: (1) clicking '+ Ekle' shows AddSteps screen; (2) typing multiple lines and pressing Cmd/Ctrl+Enter appends all non-empty lines to the queue and returns to main; (3) tapping 'Ekle' button does the same; (4) empty submit does not navigate; (5) back button navigates to main without changes; (6) the existing completion animation from Block 4 still works on the newly appended items.

data_structures.name: QueueItem
data_structures.shape: { id: string, text: string, createdAt: number }
data_structures.notes: id is crypto.randomUUID(); createdAt is Date.now() milliseconds. No `position` field in-memory — order is array index. Block 14 (localStorage) and Block 16 (Supabase) will add/map a `position` field when persisting. Do not add position here to keep the in-memory contract minimal.

data_structures.name: ViewState
data_structures.notes: Held as React useState in App.jsx. Only 'main' and 'add' are rendered in this block. The other three values are listed in a comment as reserved for future blocks. No serialisation needed (navigation state resets to 'main' on page reload, which is correct — do not persist view state).

interfaces.name: appendSteps
interfaces.purpose: Canonical contract for appending steps to the queue. Accepts pre-split, pre-trimmed, non-empty strings. Called by AddSteps (this block), by chains load (Block 12), and potentially by sync/merge (Block 18). Must be stable — callers import it from useQueue without modification.

interfaces.name: AddSteps props
interfaces.purpose: Fully controlled component — AddSteps holds only textarea text in local state and delegates all navigation and queue mutation to the parent via callbacks. This makes it testable in isolation and reusable if the router ever changes.

interfaces.name: Queue props extension
interfaces.signature: { onAddSteps: () => void } (added to existing Queue props)
interfaces.purpose: Decouples Queue from the router — Queue doesn't know about view state, it only calls a callback. Parent (App.jsx) wires this to setView('add').

acceptance_criteria: Clicking '+ Ekle' in the main view renders AddSteps.jsx full-screen and hides the queue
Pressing Cmd+Enter (macOS) or Ctrl+Enter (Windows/Linux) while the textarea has focus submits the form
Tapping the visible 'Ekle' submit button submits the form
Each non-empty line (after trim) is appended as a separate QueueItem to the queue in the order written
All new items appear in the queue on the main screen immediately after submission
Submitting a textarea containing only whitespace/blank lines is a no-op: queue unchanged, view stays on 'add'
The back button returns to 'main' without modifying the queue
Items appended via AddSteps are completable via the existing completion flow (Block 4) without modification
The view router union type is defined with all 5 values ('main' | 'add' | 'chains' | 'chain-edit' | 'auth') as a comment in App.jsx
appendSteps is exported from useQueue.js and accepts an array of strings (not raw textarea text)
verification: npm run dev — app compiles with no errors or warnings
Open app, confirm queue is visible with '+ Ekle' button in action bar
Click '+ Ekle' — AddSteps screen appears, textarea is focused
Type 'banyoya git\nsu iç\nseccadeye git' — three lines
Press Cmd/Ctrl+Enter — returns to main, three new items visible at bottom of queue
Click '+ Ekle' again, type '  \n  ' (only whitespace) — press submit — nothing changes, still on AddSteps
Click back button from AddSteps — returns to main, no new items
Complete one of the newly appended tasks — Block 4 completion animation plays correctly
Reload page — queue resets to empty (persistence not yet active, confirming Block 14 is not accidentally bundled)
npm run build — builds without type errors

questionable_decisions.decision: Navigation on submit is handled entirely by the parent via the onSubmit callback, not inside AddSteps itself
questionable_decisions.assumption: This block is short-lived scaffolding; a controlled pattern costs one extra prop but saves confusion when Block 14 or Block 18 needs to interject (e.g., persist before navigating)
alternatives: AddSteps calls onBack() internally after onSubmit() — simpler, fewer props
AddSteps receives setView directly and manages its own exit
questionable_decisions.recommendation: Keep fully controlled (parent owns navigation). The tiny overhead pays off when Block 14 wraps the submit to persist first.

questionable_decisions.decision: appendSteps accepts already-split, already-trimmed string[] rather than raw textarea text
questionable_decisions.assumption: Block 12 (chains-load) will call appendSteps with a string[] from a chain's steps array — it should not have to fake a newline-delimited string. Splitting in the caller keeps the contract clean.
alternatives: appendSteps accepts a raw multiline string and does the split/trim internally — fewer lines in AddSteps
Two overloads: appendStep(text: string) and appendSteps(lines: string[])
questionable_decisions.recommendation: Keep string[] contract. AddSteps does the split; chains call it directly. Single responsibility.

questionable_decisions.decision: View state is held in App.jsx useState, not a context or external store
questionable_decisions.assumption: With 5 views and no deep component trees needing navigation, prop drilling is manageable. Adding context now for 5 views would be over-engineering.
alternatives: React Context for view + setView — avoids prop drilling if trees deepen
A simple useReducer in App.jsx with action types for each transition — documents valid transitions explicitly
React Router (library) — overkill per PRD §6 explicit guidance
questionable_decisions.recommendation: useState in App.jsx for this block. Revisit with useReducer if the number of views grows beyond 5 or transition logic becomes complex in Block 12/13.

questionable_decisions.decision: QueueItem shape omits a `position` field; order is determined by array index
questionable_decisions.assumption: localStorage (Block 14) can serialise/deserialise in array order. Supabase (Block 16) adds a `position` column derived from array index at sync time. No in-memory reordering is planned for v1.
alternatives: Add `position: number` now so the shape matches the Supabase schema from the start — avoids a migration in Block 16
Use a linked-list structure for O(1) prepend/append
questionable_decisions.recommendation: Omit position for now; array index is simpler and sufficient. Block 16 maps index → position at upsert time. Document the mismatch in Block 16's spec.

questionable_decisions.decision: Empty-submit is a no-op with no user feedback (no toast, no error message)
questionable_decisions.assumption: ADHD users should not be shown error states for accidental empty submits — it adds cognitive load. The textarea remains visible and focused, which is sufficient feedback.
alternatives: Shake animation on the textarea to signal invalid input — clear but adds complexity
Disable the submit button when textarea is empty — prevents accidental submit entirely
Show a brief Turkish error copy 'En az bir adım gir'
questionable_decisions.recommendation: Disable the submit button when the textarea is empty (simplest approach, zero extra copy or animation). This is a trivial one-line addition and better UX than silent no-op.

open_questions: Should AddSteps clear the textarea after a successful submit, or preserve the last input in case the user wants to add similar steps again? PRD is silent; current spec clears it. Preserving it could be useful for power users but adds confusion for ADHD users who may not remember what they already submitted.

The PRD action bar mentions 'Ekle / Zincirler · Eşitle' — should the Ekle and Zincirler buttons be two separate buttons or a single compound button? Block 9 adds Ekle; Block 12 adds Zincirler. Decide layout in Block 12 to avoid having to rework Block 9's action bar.

Should the AddSteps screen support a title/label field per the Chain Edit screen pattern (PRD §3.4 is silent on this for ad-hoc steps)? Currently out of scope, but the textarea-only design may feel bare on desktop.

crypto.randomUUID() is not available in very old browsers (pre-2021). The PWA target is modern mobile — is a polyfill needed, or can we assume the deployment environment guarantees a modern browser? If a polyfill is needed, it should be added in Block 1 (project-skeleton) retroactively.
